summary: Tutte's 4-flow conjecture remains open for general bridgeless graphs. For cubic graphs the equivalent snark conjecture was announced as proved by Robertson–Sanders–Seymour–Thomas in 1999, but the complete proof is still unpublished; two published steps (2016 and 2019) establish the doublecross and structural minor cases needed for the reduction. No breakthrough on the non-cubic general case has been reported.

since_posted.title: Three-edge-colouring doublecross cubic graphs
since_posted.authors: Katherine Edwards, Daniel P. Sanders, Paul Seymour, Robin Thomas
since_posted.venue: Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B
since_posted.claim: Proves every two-edge-connected doublecross cubic graph is 3-edge-colourable, completing the doublecross case in the reduction strategy for Tutte's snark conjecture (the cubic-graph case of the 4-flow conjecture); JCTB 119 (2016) 66–95.

since_posted.title: Excluded minors in cubic graphs
since_posted.authors: Neil Robertson, Paul Seymour, Robin Thomas
since_posted.venue: Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B
since_posted.claim: Establishes a structural characterisation of cubic graphs excluding Petersen minors, serving as a key building block in the announced but still-unpublished full proof of Tutte's snark conjecture; JCTB 138 (2019) 219–285.

search_queries: Tutte 4-flow conjecture Petersen minor nowhere-zero recent progress
nowhere-zero 4-flow Petersen minor bridgeless graph conjecture solved 2010 2020 2025
"4-flow conjecture" OR "four-flow conjecture" Seymour Robertson Thomas Petersen minor
Robertson Seymour Thomas "snark conjecture" proof Petersen minor cubic graphs 2019 2016 published
notes: The Wang–Zhang paper 'Nowhere-zero 4-flow in almost Petersen-minor free graphs' (Discrete Mathematics, ~2008, ScienceDirect pii/S0012365X07009673) appeared in search results but could not be verified via WebFetch (403 errors from ScienceDirect and ResearchGate); it is excluded from since_posted. The Robertson–Seymour–Thomas arXiv preprint 1403.2118 was submitted in 2014 but published in JCTB 138 (2019) per Wikipedia; arXiv fetch did not confirm the journal. The complete snark-theorem proof (implying the cubic case of the 4-flow conjecture) has been announced since 1999 but remains unpublished as of 2026.
